However, the most potent reason
for the exposition's failure is found
in the purpose of a party Of the
original promoters to engineer a
clever real estate speculation. 1t
was figured that the exposition was
to bring improvements to the bar
rem waste of land which was
chosen for the site. and the pro-
motors We're to profit by this I'M
earned increment by becoming
owners of this land.


This Influenced the choice of the
eXposition's site, and the most I'M
portant reason for it's failure was
the inaccessibility of this site. To
reach the place boat trip from
Washington of IL hours was re-
quired, and there never has been a
time when there were enough boats
to handle the traffic comfortably.


In addition to which there were
no facilities at the exposition for
covering the grounds, and the Wis
itor was subjected from the begin
ming to all manner of petty imposi-
ton and discomfort.


A determined effort will be made
in congress to foil the efforts of the
lobby to try to make the govern
ment "a goat for this losing spec
ulatiOn.
